title: Guard compilation + schema re-elaboration
analysis:
This task implements guard compilation and schema re-elaboration for the Reify compiler and evaluation engine.

**Current state:** The parser (task 59, dependency) already produces `GuardedGroupDecl` with `condition`, `members`, `else_members`, and per-declaration `WhereClause` on `ParamDecl`, `LetDecl`, `ConstraintDecl`, `SubDecl`, `MinimizeDecl`, `MaximizeDecl`. The compiler currently has a no-op stub at line 807-810 that ignores `GuardedGroup` members. Per-declaration `where_clause` fields on decls are parsed but not used by the compiler.

**Compiler side (reify-compiler/src/lib.rs):**
1. Define `CompiledGuardedGroup` struct with guard_expr, guard_value_cell, members, constraints, else_members, else_constraints.
2. Add `guarded_groups: Vec<CompiledGuardedGroup>` and `structure_controlling: HashSet<ValueCellId>` to `TopologyTemplate`.
3. In `compile_structure`, handle `MemberDecl::GuardedGroup` by compiling guard condition into a synthetic bool ValueCell, then compiling members/constraints/else under that guard.
4. Handle per-declaration `where_clause` by creating single-member guarded groups.
5. Handle nested guards via conjunctive composition (inner = outer && inner_cond).
6. Handle else blocks by populating else_members/else_constraints.
7. Implement reference safety: detect unguarded references to guarded ValueCells.

**Eval side (reify-eval/src/graph.rs, lib.rs, snapshot.rs):**
1. Extend `EvaluationGraph::from_templates()` to process guarded_groups: create guard ValueCell nodes, store GuardedGroupInfo metadata.
2. In `Engine::eval()`, evaluate guard ValueCells first, then conditionally evaluate guarded/else members.
3. Implement schema re-elaboration: when a structure-controlling guard changes in `edit_param()`, re-evaluate affected members.
4. Handle Undef guards (members in indeterminate state).
5. Update topology fingerprint to include guard states.

**Key patterns observed in codebase:**
- Compiler uses two-pass compilation: first pass registers names in scope (line 655-694), second pass compiles expressions (line 697-811).
- `compile_structure()` iterates over `structure.members` matching on `MemberDecl` variants.
- `EvaluationGraph::from_templates()` (graph.rs:68-141) iterates over templates and creates typed nodes.
- Tests use parse-from-source or builder-constructed modules.
- TopologyTemplateBuilder provides fluent API for test module construction.
- CompilationScope maps names -> (ValueCellId, Type); needs extension for guard tracking.
- Existing `std::collections::HashMap` import at compiler line 1; `HashSet` needs to be added.
- Existing `ValueCellKind` has Param/Let/Auto variants.

prerequisites.description: Add CompiledGuardedGroup struct and extend TopologyTemplate with guarded_groups: Vec<CompiledGuardedGroup> and structure_controlling: HashSet<ValueCellId> fields in reify-compiler/src/lib.rs. Add 'use std::collections::HashSet' import. Update compile_structure() to initialize empty guarded_groups/structure_controlling. Update content_hash computation to include guarded group hashes. Update TopologyTemplateBuilder.build() in reify-test-support/src/builders.rs to initialize new fields (empty defaults). Ensure all existing tests still compile.

Add guarded_group() builder method to TopologyTemplateBuilder in reify-test-support/src/builders.rs. Method accepts guard_expr (CompiledExpr), guard_value_cell (ValueCellId), members (Vec<ValueCellDecl>), constraints (Vec<CompiledConstraint>), else_members (Vec<ValueCellDecl>), else_constraints (Vec<CompiledConstraint>) and appends to internal guarded_groups vec and structure_controlling set. Update build() content_hash to include guarded groups.

Add GuardedGroupInfo struct and guarded_groups: Vec<GuardedGroupInfo>, structure_controlling: HashSet<ValueCellId> fields to EvaluationGraph in reify-eval/src/graph.rs. GuardedGroupInfo holds: guard_cell (ValueCellId), members (Vec<ValueCellId>), constraints (Vec<ConstraintNodeId>), else_members (Vec<ValueCellId>), else_constraints (Vec<ConstraintNodeId>). Update topology_fingerprint() to include guard-related hashes. Initialize fields empty in Default.

Create empty test files: crates/reify-compiler/tests/guard_compilation.rs and crates/reify-eval/tests/guard_eval.rs with basic imports from reify_compiler, reify_test_support, reify_types, reify_eval. Verify cargo test --no-run succeeds.

steps.description: Write failing test in guard_compilation.rs: compile_param_with_where_clause. Parse `structure S { param active : Bool = true\n param x : Scalar = 5mm where active }` via reify_syntax::parse then reify_compiler::compile. Assert TopologyTemplate.guarded_groups has 1 entry with guard_expr referencing 'active', members contains one ValueCellDecl for x, and guard_value_cell is in structure_controlling. Assert x is NOT in the top-level value_cells.

Implement per-declaration guard compilation in compile_structure(). In both passes: check where_clause on ParamDecl/LetDecl/ConstraintDecl. In pass 1, still register guarded names in scope (so they can be referenced). In pass 2: (1) compile guard condition via compile_expr, (2) create synthetic guard ValueCellId (entity, '__guard_N'), (3) create guard ValueCellDecl (Bool, Let, default_expr=guard_condition), (4) create CompiledGuardedGroup with the member, (5) do NOT add guarded member to top-level value_cells, (6) add guard_value_cell to structure_controlling. Use a guard_index counter for unique naming.

Write failing test: compile_block_guard. Parse `structure S { param active : Bool = true
 where active { param x : Scalar = 5mm
 param y : Scalar = 10mm
 constraint x > 2mm } }`. Assert guarded_groups has 1 entry with 2 members (x, y ValueCellDecls) and 1 constraint under the same guard.

Implement block guard compilation. Replace the no-op GuardedGroup(_) arm in compile_structure(). When encountering MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(g): (1) compile g.condition, (2) create synthetic guard ValueCell, (3) iterate g.members compiling each Param/Let into CompiledGuardedGroup.members and Constraint into CompiledGuardedGroup.constraints, (4) add guard_value_cell to structure_controlling. Register block-guarded names in pass 1 as well.

Write failing test: compile_nested_guards. Parse `structure S { param a : Bool = true\n param b : Bool = true\n where a { where b { param x : Scalar = 1mm } } }`. Assert the inner guarded group's guard_expr is a BinOp::And referencing the outer guard ValueCell and inner condition 'b'. Assert 2 entries in guarded_groups (one per nesting level) or 1 entry with conjunctive guard.

Implement nested guard conjunction. Refactor guard compilation into a helper function that accepts Option<ValueCellId> for outer_guard. When outer_guard is Some, the inner guard_expr = CompiledExpr::binop(BinOp::And, ValueRef(outer_guard), inner_condition, Type::Bool). When compiling nested GuardedGroupDecl members, pass the current guard ValueCellId as outer_guard context.

Write failing test: compile_else_block. Parse `structure S { param cond : Bool = true\n where cond { param a : Scalar = 1mm } else { param b : Scalar = 2mm } }`. Assert guarded_groups[0] has members=[a ValueCellDecl] and else_members=[b ValueCellDecl], sharing the same guard_value_cell.

Implement else block compilation. When GuardedGroupDecl has non-empty else_members, compile each into CompiledGuardedGroup.else_members and else_constraints. Register else-block names in pass 1 for scope resolution. Else members are active when guard=false -- the eval engine handles conditional logic.

Write failing test: reference_safety_unguarded_to_guarded_error. Parse `structure S { param active : Bool = true\n param x : Scalar = 5mm where active\n let y = x }`. Unguarded let y references guarded x. Compile should produce a diagnostic error containing 'unguarded reference' or 'guarded'.

Implement reference safety checking. Extend CompilationScope.names to map name -> (ValueCellId, Type, Option<ValueCellId>) where the third element is the guard cell ID. Update register() to accept optional guard_cell. In compile_expr() for Ident, when resolved cell has a guard and current compilation context has no guard or weaker guard, push Diagnostic::error about unsafe reference. Pass guard context through compile of guarded group members.

Write test: reference_safety_same_guard_ok. Parse `structure S { param active : Bool = true\n where active { param x : Scalar = 5mm\n let y = x } }`. Both x and y are under the same guard 'active'. Compile should produce no reference safety error diagnostics.

Ensure same-guard and stronger-guard references pass validation. When both referencing context and referenced cell share the same guard_value_cell ID, no error is emitted. For conjunction guards (step-6), check if the referenced cell's guard ID appears in the referencing context's guard chain. This may already work from step-10; verify and fix edge cases.

Write failing test in guard_eval.rs: eval_guard_true_includes_members. Build a CompiledModule via builders: Bool param 'active' with default true, guarded_group with guard_expr=ValueRef(active), guard_value_cell='S.__guard_0', member param 'x' with default 5mm. Create Engine with MockConstraintChecker, call eval(). Assert 'x' value in result is 0.005 (5mm SI).

Implement guard processing in EvaluationGraph::from_templates(). For each template.guarded_groups: (1) create ValueCellNode for guard_value_cell (Bool, Let, default_expr=guard_expr), (2) create ValueCellNodes for all members and else_members, (3) create ConstraintNodeData for all constraints and else_constraints, (4) store GuardedGroupInfo, (5) add guard_value_cell to structure_controlling. In Engine::eval(), evaluate guard ValueCells during the Let evaluation pass (they're Let kind), then evaluate guarded members only when guard evaluates to true (Bool(true)).

Write failing test: eval_guard_false_includes_else. Build template with Bool param 'active' default=false, guarded_group with member param 'x'=5mm and else_member param 'y'=10mm. After eval, assert 'y' value is 0.01 (10mm SI), and 'x' value is Undef.

Implement conditional member evaluation in Engine::eval(). After evaluating guard cells: check EvaluationGraph.guarded_groups. For each group, look up guard_cell value. If true: evaluate members, set else_members to Undef. If false: evaluate else_members, set members to Undef. Update snapshot values with appropriate DeterminacyState.

Write failing test: eval_guard_undef_members_indeterminate. Build template where guard references an Auto param (starts Undef, no solver). After eval, guard cell is Undef. Assert all guarded members have Undef values and DeterminacyState::Undetermined in the snapshot.

Implement Undef guard handling. When guard evaluates to non-Bool (Undef or other), treat guard as indeterminate: include guarded members in graph with Undef values and DeterminacyState::Undetermined. Do not evaluate else_members either (both branches indeterminate). This is the initial state before guard resolution.

Write failing test: guard_change_triggers_re_elaboration. Engine::eval() with guard=true (member x present, value=5mm). Then edit_param to change 'active' Bool from true to false. Assert: (1) topology_fingerprint changed between snapshots, (2) x value is Undef in result, (3) else_member y is present with evaluated value, (4) structure_controlling cell detected the change.

Implement schema re-elaboration in Engine::edit_param(). After evaluating dirty nodes, check if any structure_controlling cells changed boolean value. If so: (1) re-evaluate guard ValueCells, (2) determine new active/inactive members from guarded_groups, (3) evaluate newly-activated members, (4) set newly-deactivated members to Undef, (5) recompute topology_fingerprint on the snapshot including current guard states. Ensure the dirty cone and eval set include dependents of affected guarded members.

design_decisions.decision: Synthetic guard ValueCells use naming convention '{entity}.__guard_{index}' with Bool type and Let kind
design_decisions.rationale: Guard expressions need to be evaluated like any other expression in the graph. Using synthetic ValueCells with a double-underscore naming convention avoids collision with user-defined names (Reify identifiers don't start with underscores). Let kind is appropriate since guards are computed, not user-settable. This allows reuse of all existing evaluation infrastructure.

design_decisions.decision: Per-declaration where clauses desugar to single-member CompiledGuardedGroups
design_decisions.rationale: Treating per-declaration guards and block guards uniformly simplifies the eval engine -- it only handles CompiledGuardedGroup. The compiler does the desugaring. This matches the task description: 'create a CompiledGuardedGroup with one member per guard expression'.

design_decisions.decision: Nested guards compose via conjunction (AND) into a single guard per nesting level
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec: 'inner guard = outer_guard && inner_condition'. Each nesting level gets its own synthetic guard ValueCell whose default_expr is AND(ValueRef(outer_guard_cell), inner_condition). The inner members reference the inner guard cell, which transitively depends on the outer.

design_decisions.decision: Else members stored in CompiledGuardedGroup.else_members rather than creating separate negated guard groups
design_decisions.rationale: The guard_value_cell evaluates to true/false. The eval engine selects members when true, else_members when false. No extra negated guard cell needed. Task spec: 'desugar to where !condition'.

design_decisions.decision: Reference safety uses guard ValueCellId equality for v0.1
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec: 'For v0.1, stronger means syntactic equality or conjunction containing the guard'. Comparing guard_value_cell IDs is sufficient. Full semantic guard implication analysis deferred.

design_decisions.decision: Guard context tracked in CompilationScope as Option<ValueCellId> per registered name
design_decisions.rationale: Reference safety checking during expression compilation needs to know each cell's guard. Extending the scope map from (ValueCellId, Type) to (ValueCellId, Type, Option<ValueCellId>) is minimal and enables O(1) lookup.

design_decisions.decision: All guarded members exist in EvaluationGraph but are conditionally evaluated
design_decisions.rationale: Rather than dynamically adding/removing graph nodes, all potential members (both guard-true and guard-false branches) are added to the graph by from_templates(). The eval engine skips inactive members (setting them to Undef). This simplifies dependency tracking and dirty-cone computation. Re-elaboration changes which members are active/inactive, not graph structure.

design_decisions.decision: Topology fingerprint encodes guard states by including guard cell values
design_decisions.rationale: When a guard changes from true to false, the effective topology changes (different members are active). The fingerprint must reflect this so that downstream consumers (LSP, geometry kernel) detect the schema change. Including guard cell values in the fingerprint (alongside structural hashes) achieves this.

reuse.what: compile_expr() function for guard condition compilation
reuse.how: Reuse directly to compile guard condition expressions from WhereClause and GuardedGroupDecl.condition into CompiledExpr. Already handles Ident, BinOp (&&, ||), UnOp (!), Bool literals.
Extend the names map from (ValueCellId, Type) to (ValueCellId, Type, Option<ValueCellId>) to track guard context. Minimal change to existing API.
Reuse directly as member types within CompiledGuardedGroup. No changes needed to these types.
Extend with guarded_group() method following same fluent pattern. Existing param/let_binding/constraint methods serve as patterns.
Use for nested guard conjunction (And) and guard condition references (ValueRef to guard cells).
Extend existing for-loop over templates to also process template.guarded_groups, creating guard/member ValueCellNodes and constraint ConstraintNodeData.
Existing pattern of iterating graph.value_cells to initialize (Undef, Undetermined) will automatically cover guard ValueCells once they're in the graph.

reuse.what: MockConstraintChecker for Engine construction in tests
reuse.how: All eval guard tests use MockConstraintChecker::new() as the constraint checker, following existing eval test patterns.

reuse.what: Expression builder helpers (literal, value_ref, value_ref_typed, and, not)
reuse.how: Use for constructing guard condition expressions and test assertions.
Include guarded group hashes and guard state hashes in fingerprint computation.
Guard cells are evaluated in pass 2 (Let bindings). Add a guard-awareness phase between pass 2 and sub-component elaboration to conditionally evaluate guarded members.
